title: Podman in Kubernetes can not mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay flags: 0x1000: permission denied
body:
**Description**
I am switching our CI pipelines from VM to Kubernetes (later to OpenShift).
One of the tasks is to build and run images during the pipeline run.

We use IBM Cloud Kubernetes service.
I followed the steps in the article. 

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes

Podman runs without problem in :
- Rootful Podman with the privileged flag set
- Rootless Podman with the privileged flag set

Podman does not run:
- **Rootful Podman without the privileged flag (current working on)**
- Rootless Podman without the privileged flag (next step in improving security)


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. We use IBM Cloud Kubernetes services. 
```
$ kubectl get nodes -o wide
NAME             STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION       INTERNAL-IP      EXTERNAL-IP       OS-IMAGE             KERNEL-VERSION       CONTAINER-RUNTIME
10.135.230.178   Ready    <none>   8d    v1.24.2+IKS   10.135.230.178   158.17*****70   Ubuntu 18.04.6 LTS   4.15.0-188-generic   containerd://1.6.6
10.135.230.186   Ready    <none>   8d    v1.24.2+IKS   10.135.230.186   169.5*****1      Ubuntu 18.04.6 LTS   4.15.0-188-generic   containerd://1.6.6
10.135.230.188   Ready    <none>   8d    v1.24.2+IKS   10.135.230.188   158.17*****1   Ubuntu 18.04.6 LTS   4.15.0-188-generic   containerd://1.6.6
```

2. I installed device plugin `fuse-device-plugin-daemonset`
```
$ kubectl -n kube-system logs fuse-device-plugin-daemonset-b6mpq
2022/07/20 21:10:33 Starting
2022/07/20 21:10:33 Starting FS watcher.
2022/07/20 21:10:33 Starting OS watcher.
2022/07/20 21:10:33 Starting to serve on /var/lib/kubelet/device-plugins/fuse.sock
2022/07/20 21:10:33 Registered device plugin with Kubelet
```

3.  Install rootful-no-priv.yaml from the documentation https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes :
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
 name: no-priv-rootful
spec:
 containers:
   - name: no-priv-rootful
     image: quay.io/podman/stable
     args:
       - sleep
       - "1000000"
     securityContext:
       capabilities:
         add:
           - "SYS_ADMIN"
           - "MKNOD"
           - "SYS_CHROOT"
           - "SETFCAP"
           - "SYS_RESOURCE"
     resources:
       limits:
         github.com/fuse: 1
```

**Describe the results you received:**
And here is the error.
```
$ kubectl exec -it no-priv-rootful -- bash
[root@no-priv-rootful /]# ls -l /dev/
total 0
...
crw-rw-rw- 1 root root  1,   7 Jul 20 21:47 full
crw-rw-rw- 1 root root 10, 229 Jul 20 21:47 fuse
drwxrwxrwt 2 root root      40 Jul 20 21:47 mqueue
...
[root@no-priv-rootful /]# podman info
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
[root@no-priv-rootful /]# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
[root@no-priv-rootful /]# podman run ubi8 echo hello
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

It builds/runs the container.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
```

As the image is the same, I took the output from from privilege container.
```
1# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 22 16:17:44 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug
Error: mount /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay:/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
```

As the image is the same, I took the output from from privilege container.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**
Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Might be a issue in AppArmor that IBM Cloud enforce on the kubernetes node?
Based on the documentaion:

https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-security
Expert AppArmor policies
Every worker node is set up with security and access policies that are enforced by [AppArmor](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/AppArmor) profiles that are loaded into the worker node during bootstrapping. AppArmor profiles can't be changed by the user or owner of the machine.

Can you suggest what can be an issue?
And maybe some tips how to solve it?

comments.body: Is this server using SECCOMP?  This might be a SECCOMP filter blocking the mount syscall?

Containerd's [apparmor profile](https://github.com/containerd/containerd/blob/main/contrib/apparmor/template.go#L76) is denying the `mount` syscall.

If you cannot change this profile, I suggest to try running with disabling apparmor.  It's less secure but better than running a privileged container.

I am closing the issue but we can continue the conversation here.

Thank you @vrothberg @rhatdan  for the comments. **The issue was in the AppArmor profile.**
I tried to disabled it completely but after reboot, the node could not connect to master and was always in "Not Ready" state.

Then I start adding profiles into complain mode. But I could not find the _cri-containerd.apparmor.d_ in the /etc/apparmor.d and other system directories.
The result what I could reach.
```
root@kube-bpf5jetf0mk4ld6hbfrg-jenkinsclus-removea-00000c35:/etc/apparmor.d# aa-status
apparmor module is loaded.
17 profiles are loaded.
3 profiles are in enforce mode.
   cri-containerd.apparmor.d
   man_filter
   man_groff
14 profiles are in complain mode.
   /sbin/dhclient
   /usr/bin/man
....
```

The solution for the problem is in the article https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/security/apparmor/
There is corresponding annotation to disable apparmor for the container.

```
 annotations:
  container.apparmor.security.beta.kubernetes.io/<container name>: unconfined
```
After that I could build and run container for my use case:
- Rootful Podman without the privileged flag
- Rootless Podman without the privileged flag

As a next I will try to run podman in openshift in without the privileged flag. Do you have any tips how to run it there?

@rhatdan @vrothberg 
Im stuck with `Rootful Podman without the privileged flag` in OpenShift.

I can build the image but i can not run
Here is manifest of the pod and error:

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: no-priv-rootful
spec:
  serviceAccount: rootful-no-priv-openshift
  containers:
    - name: no-priv-rootful
      image: quay.io/podman/stable
      args:
        - sleep
        - "1000000"
      securityContext:
        capabilities:
          add:
            - "SYS_ADMIN"
            - "MKNOD"
            - "SYS_CHROOT"
            - "SETFCAP"
            - "SYS_RESOURCE"
            - "SETUID"
            - "SETGID"
      resources:
        limits:
          github.com/fuse: 1
```
inside the pod
```

export BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
```

It is something similar to AppArmor in Ubuntu but in this case on the SE-Linux level.
Do you have ideas how to allow the mount in the pod?

@umohnani8 PTAL
@oshamin You could try running the container as the user podman (1000) inside of the container.

@rhatdan 
Added: 
```
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
```

The error is the same. (I used OpenShift in IBM Cloud)
```
$ oc exec -it no-priv-rootful -- sh
sh-5.1$ id
uid=1000(podman) gid=1000(podman) groups=1000(podman)
sh-5.1$ export _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS=""
export BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
export STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs
sh-5.1$ podman run ubi8 echo hello
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob 7e3624512448 done
Copying blob db0f4cd41250 done
Copying config f754849582 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: crun: set propagation for `proc`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
sh-5.1$
```
Could you run the container with an SELinux type of spc_t, to see if this is an SELinux error, or better yet check the audit.log on the OpenSHift node to see if there are AVC messages.

I tried the following securityContext and it works for containers with root and for podman user.
```
      securityContext
        seLinuxOptions:
          type: spc_t
```
The audit log is empty during the `podman run ...`. so the audit is only logged what is denied.

Also with this setup It works without setting environment variables.
```
export _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS=""
export BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot 
export STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs
```

Here is successful run
```
$ oc exec -it no-priv-rootful -- sh
sh-5.1$ id
uid=1000(podman) gid=1000(podman) groups=1000(podman)
sh-5.1$ podman run ubi8 echo hello
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob 7e3624512448 done
Copying blob db0f4cd41250 done
Copying config f754849582 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
hello
```
